title: Fix a typo in batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap
body:
The current implementation can cause `reproj_mask` to be undefined in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap()` if `max_reproj_error = None` causing the following: 

```
if masks is not None and reproj_mask is not None:
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'reproj_mask' referenced before assignment
``` 

Initializing `reproj_mask` beforehand fix that error.

step_plan.summary: Fixes an `UnboundLocalError` in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap` by ensuring `reproj_mask` is always defined (initialized to `None`) before it is conditionally computed and later referenced during mask merging.

steps.operation: Initialize reproj_mask to None before the max_reproj_error conditional and keep existing mask-merge logic intact
steps.reason: reproj_mask is currently only defined inside the `if max_reproj_error is not None:` block but is referenced afterward in `if masks is not None and reproj_mask is not None:`; setting `reproj_mask = None` up-front prevents `UnboundLocalError` when `max_reproj_error=None` while preserving the intended semantics (“no reprojection filtering applied”).
steps.side_effects: Behavioral change is limited to control-flow safety: when `max_reproj_error=None`, reprojection filtering is skipped and `reproj_mask` remains None, so provided `masks` are used as-is; if neither `masks` nor `max_reproj_error` are provided, the existing `assert masks is not None` will still fail (now without an earlier UnboundLocalError). Consider adding/adjusting a unit test or minimal regression snippet to cover `max_reproj_error=None` with `masks` provided.
